mainIndustries.zh: 稻米、黍类、甘蔗、豆类和油料作物种植；桑蚕与丝绸；棉纺与织布；檀香、木材与林产采集；制糖、榨油、陶器、金属手工、象牙与木工艺；畜牧及地区贸易运输
mainIndustries.en: Cultivation of rice, millets, sugarcane, pulses, and oilseeds; sericulture and silk; cotton spinning and weaving; sandalwood, timber, and forest extraction; sugar making, oil pressing, pottery, metal crafts, ivory and woodwork; livestock and regional transport trade

tradeGoods.zh: 输出稻米、糖、油料、纺织品、丝绸、檀香木、木材、牲畜和地方手工艺品；输入金属制品、盐、部分奢侈品、军需物资、欧洲制造品及经英属印度转运的国际商品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, sugar, oilseeds, textiles, silk, sandalwood, timber, livestock, and local handicrafts; imports included metal goods, salt, certain luxuries, military supplies, European manufactures, and internationally traded goods routed through British India

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富高度依赖土地、牲畜、收成和村社资源。多数农村家庭仅有有限余粮与小额银币储蓄，富裕程度差异显著；地主、税务中间人、商人和宫廷服务阶层拥有更多可变现资产、珠宝和地租收益。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended heavily on land, livestock, harvests, and village resources. Most rural households possessed only limited surplus grain and small silver savings, with strong variation in prosperity; landlords, revenue intermediaries, merchants, and court service groups controlled greater liquid assets, jewelry, and rent income.

economicSystem.zh: 以土地税驱动的农业君主制经济，结合地方市场交换、手工业生产、寺庙与宫廷再分配，以及在英国政治经济框架下逐渐扩张的商品化贸易
economicSystem.en: An agrarian monarchical economy driven by land revenue, combined with local market exchange, handicraft production, temple and court redistribution, and gradually expanding commercialization within the political economy of British India

source.en: Customs, market dues, and transit duties

source.zh: 王室产业、林产与垄断收入
source.en: Royal estates, forest income, and monopolies

source.zh: 司法罚金、执照费及杂项
source.en: Judicial fines, license fees, and miscellaneous income

category.zh: 向英国支付的补助与政治义务支出
category.en: Payments to the British and political obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况总体稳健，能够维持宫廷、地方行政与常备治安开支，并保留一定储备以应对歉收、英方追加要求或地方动荡。但财政自主性受英国监督及对外主权受限所削弱。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was generally sound, able to sustain court, administrative, and standing security expenses while retaining a modest reserve against poor harvests, additional British demands, or local disturbances. However, fiscal autonomy was weakened by British oversight and restricted external sovereignty.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持稳定征收、避免激烈税改、优先确保地税收入和政治性付款为核心，同时有限投入灌溉修复与道路改善，以保持农业产出和社会秩序。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on stable collection, avoidance of drastic tax reform, and prioritization of land revenue and politically necessary payments, while devoting limited funds to irrigation repair and road improvement in order to preserve agricultural output and social order.

description.zh: 以地方招募和旧式编制为基础，部分单位接受英印式操练，装备火绳枪、燧发枪及冷兵器，主要承担守备和内部治安任务。
description.en: Based on local recruitment and older formations, with some units drilled in the manner of British India. They were equipped with matchlocks, flintlocks, and edged weapons, and primarily served in garrison and internal security roles.

description.zh: 规模较小，多用于传令、巡逻、护卫和快速镇压地方骚乱，而非进行大规模野战冲击。
description.en: Relatively small in number, used chiefly for dispatch, patrol, escort, and rapid response to local unrest rather than large-scale battlefield shock.

description.zh: 保有若干火炮和训练有素的炮兵骨干，但总体规模和技术水平均受英方限制，更多用于要地防守与礼仪展示。
description.en: Maintained a number of guns and a trained artillery cadre, but overall scale and technical development were limited by British restrictions; artillery was used mainly for key-point defense and ceremonial display.

description.zh: 由各地警备、城防和临时召集人员构成，在需要时支援治安、护税和道路巡防。
description.en: Comprised local guards, town defense contingents, and temporarily mobilized men, supporting policing, tax protection, and road patrol when needed.

commanderInChief.zh: 迈索尔大君主名义统帅，实际军事事务受迪万与英国驻扎官监督
commanderInChief.en: The Maharaja was nominal commander-in-chief, while practical military affairs were supervised by the Diwan and the British Resident

technology.zh: 军事技术处于火器时代的中低水平，以旧式火枪、少量较新燧发枪、传统炮兵和冷兵器并用。与提普苏丹时期相比，军事实验性和独立军工能力明显下降，且不得建立足以挑战英方的现代化军队。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a lower-middle firearms-era level, combining older muskets, some newer flintlocks, traditional artillery, and edged weapons. Compared with the era of Tipu Sultan, military experimentation and independent war-making capacity had declined sharply, and Mysore was not permitted to develop a modern army capable of challenging British power.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非外部征服战争，而是英国对内部治理失误的干预、地方税务骚乱、歉收导致的治安问题，以及在英印政治格局中被进一步削弱自主权的风险。
threats.en: The chief threats were not external conquest wars but British intervention in case of maladministration, local revenue unrest, law-and-order problems arising from bad harvests, and the risk of further erosion of autonomy within the political framework of British India.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 套筒刺刀，仍为步兵白刃战标配
description.en: Socket bayonet, still standard for infantry close combat

description.zh: 柯尔特型转轮手枪，可连发六弹
description.en: Colt-type revolver capable of firing six rounds

description.zh: 骑兵军刀仍在使用
description.en: Cavalry saber still in active use

populationDescription.zh: 人口约三百万，以农村居民为绝大多数，分布于高原耕作区、灌溉带、林地边缘与若干城镇中心。人口密度在可耕作与水利条件较好的地区较高，受季风和农业周期影响明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 3 million, overwhelmingly rural and distributed across plateau farming zones, irrigated tracts, forest margins, and a limited number of towns. Population density was higher in arable and well-watered areas, and demographic life was strongly shaped by the monsoon and agricultural cycles.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、婆罗门文官与祭司、地主和税收中间人、商人与放贷者、手工业者、农民、佃户、牧民、劳工及受压迫种姓群体共同构成社会阶层。阶层身份与种姓、职业、土地占有和宗教赞助密切相关。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal family and nobles, Brahmin officials and priests, landlords and revenue intermediaries, merchants and moneylenders, artisans, peasants, tenants, pastoralists, laborers, and oppressed caste communities. Social rank was closely tied to caste, occupation, landholding, and access to religious patronage.

allies.zh: 无真正独立意义上的正式盟友；主要政治依附对象为英属印度当局，与周边南印度土邦保持谨慎而从属的往来
allies.en: No fully independent allies in the sovereign sense; its primary political connection was with the authorities of British India, while it maintained cautious and subordinate relations with neighboring South Indian princely states

enemies.zh: 1840年无公开敌国；潜在摩擦主要来自英国对其内政和财政的监督压力，以及与邻近行政辖区在边界、税务和司法事务上的零星争议
enemies.en: There was no open enemy state in 1840; potential friction arose chiefly from British pressure over internal administration and finance, as well as occasional disputes with neighboring jurisdictions over boundaries, taxation, and legal matters

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策极为有限，重心在于维持王朝地位、避免触怒英国、保持内部稳定并争取在宗教文化和地方行政领域保留尽可能多的自主空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was extremely limited, focusing on preserving the dynasty, avoiding conflict with the British, maintaining internal stability, and retaining as much autonomy as possible in religious, cultural, and local administrative affairs.

era.zh: 工业革命时代下的前工业南亚土邦
era.en: A pre-industrial South Asian princely state in the age of the Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非自主工业突破，而是土地丈量、账册行政、道路与灌溉修缮、丝绸与手工业技术延续，以及通过英属印度接触到的近代测绘、火器操练和文书规范。相较欧洲工业国家，机械化和工厂化极为有限。
keyInnovations.en: Its key technical features were not autonomous industrial breakthroughs but improved land survey, bookkeeping administration, road and irrigation repair, continuity in silk and handicraft techniques, and growing exposure through British India to modern surveying, firearms drill, and documentary standardization. Compared with industrial Europe, mechanization and factory production were extremely limited.

strengths.zh: 农业基础扎实、王朝合法性较强、文化传统深厚、财政总体尚稳、地方治理网络成熟，且在英国保护伞下免于大规模外敌入侵。
strengths.en: Its strengths included a solid agricultural base, strong dynastic legitimacy, deep cultural traditions, generally stable finances, mature local governance networks, and protection from large-scale foreign invasion under British dominance.

weaknesses.zh: 主权受限、军事自主性低、经济结构单一、社会等级与种姓分化明显、基础设施现代化不足，且一旦被指控治理失当即可能遭受更严厉的英国接管。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited sovereignty, low military autonomy, a narrow agrarian economic structure, pronounced social and caste inequality, insufficient infrastructural modernization, and vulnerability to stricter British takeover if accused of maladministration.

outlook.zh: 短期内迈索尔有望维持稳定与王朝延续，但其未来取决于能否在英国监督下改善财政和行政表现。若治理被认定失效，王权可能进一步被架空；若能保持秩序并适度改革，则仍可作为重要南印度土邦延续存在。
outlook.en: In the short term Mysore was likely to remain stable and preserve dynastic continuity, but its future depended on improving fiscal and administrative performance under British supervision. If governance were judged ineffective, royal authority could be further hollowed out; if order were maintained and moderate reform pursued, it could continue as an important South Indian princely state.

description.zh: 南印度重要土邦，在英方监控下维持传统王朝统治。
description.en: An important South Indian princely state maintaining traditional dynastic rule under British oversight.

governmentForm.zh: 君主制，名义上为中央集权王国，实际上受部族联盟、地方首领与英国军事支持共同制约
governmentForm.en: Monarchy, nominally a centralized kingdom but in practice constrained by tribal coalitions, local chiefs, and British military backing

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族、宗族与地方共同体为核心，上层为王室、萨多宰与巴拉克宰等贵族家族、宗教学者与部族汗，下层为牧民、农民、手工业者与商人，城市人口比例较低
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on tribes, kinship groups, and local communities. The upper strata included the royal house, noble Durrani lineages such as the Sadozai and Barakzai, religious scholars, and tribal khans, while the lower strata consisted of pastoralists, peasants, artisans, and merchants; urban society remained limited

rulingClass.zh: 杜兰尼王室、普什图部族贵族、地方汗与伊斯兰宗教精英
rulingClass.en: The Durrani royal house, Pashtun tribal nobility, regional khans, and Islamic religious elites

succession.zh: 王位原则上实行王朝世袭，但常因宗族竞争、部族支持与武力夺位而反复争夺
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the dynasty, but in practice succession was frequently contested through clan rivalry, tribal backing, and armed seizure

structure.zh: 1840年的阿富汗政权为围绕喀布尔宫廷运作的君主政体。中央朝廷对喀布尔及周边地区控制较强，但对坎大哈、加兹尼、赫拉特以外广阔山地和部族地带的支配十分有限。英国驻军、使节与财政支援在沙阿·舒贾复位后对政务产生重大影响
structure.en: The Afghan polity in 1840 was a monarchical regime operating around the court at Kabul. The central court exercised stronger control over Kabul and nearby districts, but its authority over Kandahar, Ghazni, and the wider mountain and tribal zones was highly limited. After Shah Shuja’s restoration, British garrisons, envoys, and financial support significantly influenced governance

name.zh: 王室宫廷与御前秘书处

function.zh: 处理诏令、接见、王室财政分配、任命文书与与英国代表往来
function.en: Handled decrees, audiences, royal financial allocations, appointment documents, and correspondence with British representatives

function.zh: 征收土地税、市场税、关卡税及向地方首领索取贡赋，管理王室收入
function.en: Collected land taxes, market dues, transit levies, and tribute from local chiefs, and managed royal revenue

function.zh: 联络地方总督、部族汗与边地首领，协调效忠、驻军与纠纷调停
function.en: Maintained relations with provincial governors, tribal khans, and frontier chiefs, coordinating allegiance, garrisons, and dispute mediation

function.zh: 任命卡齐与宗教法官，处理婚姻、继承、商业与刑事案件中的教法事务
function.en: Appointed qadis and religious judges and handled sharia matters in marriage, inheritance, commerce, and criminal cases

function.zh: 统筹王室亲军、地方征召、堡垒补给与对英军协同行动
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, local levies, fortress supply, and cooperative operations with British forces

function.zh: 管理清真寺、宗教学校、瓦克夫地产与乌里玛关系
function.en: Managed mosques, madrasas, waqf properties, and relations with the ulama

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖总督、城堡长官、税吏和部族中介人。许多地区并无稳定常设官僚体系，而是通过向部族领袖发放俸金、承认其自治权与临时谈判来维持服从
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on governors, fortress commanders, tax collectors, and tribal intermediaries. Many regions lacked a stable standing bureaucracy and were governed through stipends to tribal leaders, recognition of local autonomy, and ad hoc negotiation

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派哈乃斐学派伊斯兰教法为基础，并与部族习惯法并行。王室法令、地方惯例和部族仲裁共同构成实际司法秩序
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Sunni Hanafi Islamic law and operated alongside tribal customary law. Royal decrees, local custom, and tribal arbitration together formed the practical judicial order

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要包括农业土地税、灌溉地与园圃征课、牲畜税、商税、市场税与山口关税。由于战争与地方离心，征收极不均衡，许多收入依赖临时贡赋和外部补贴
taxationSystem.en: Taxation consisted mainly of agricultural land taxes, assessments on irrigated land and orchards, livestock taxes, customs dues, market tolls, and mountain pass transit charges. Because of war and local fragmentation, collection was highly uneven, and much revenue depended on ad hoc tribute and outside subsidies

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教占绝对主导，主要为逊尼派哈乃斐学派，亦有什叶派群体如部分哈扎拉和奇兹尔巴什社群。宗教生活深受清真寺、乌里玛、苏菲网络和圣裔家族影响
religion.en: Islam overwhelmingly predominated, chiefly Sunni of the Hanafi school, though Shi'a communities also existed, including among some Hazaras and Qizilbash groups. Religious life was deeply shaped by mosques, the ulama, Sufi networks, and families claiming sacred descent

philosophy.zh: 思想基础以伊斯兰政治伦理、部族荣誉观念、宗教法学和苏菲虔敬传统为核心。国家认同仍弱于王朝忠诚、宗教共同体认同与部族归属
philosophy.en: The intellectual foundation centered on Islamic political ethics, tribal codes of honor, religious jurisprudence, and Sufi devotional traditions. State identity remained weaker than dynastic loyalty, religious community, and tribal affiliation

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为改造后的波斯-阿拉伯字母，用于波斯语和普什图语行政、诗歌与书信；阿拉伯语则用于宗教学术文本
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the modified Perso-Arabic script, used for Persian and Pashto in administration, poetry, and correspondence; Arabic was employed for religious scholarship

culturalAchievements.zh: 19世纪初阿富汗文化成就主要体现在波斯语宫廷文学、普什图诗歌、宗教教育、书法和地方建筑传统上。喀布尔、坎大哈与赫拉特虽受战乱影响，仍保持区域性文人和商旅文化交流
culturalAchievements.en: Afghan cultural achievements in the early nineteenth century were expressed mainly in Persian courtly literature, Pashto poetry, religious education, calligraphy, and regional architectural traditions. Kabul, Kandahar, and Herat, though affected by warfare, still maintained regional circuits of learned and mercantile cultural exchange

languageFamily.zh: 以伊朗语族语言为主，达里波斯语为宫廷和行政通用语，普什图语为主要部族与政治语言之一，另有突厥语和其他地方语言
languageFamily.en: Iranian languages predominated: Dari Persian served as the courtly and administrative lingua franca, while Pashto was one of the principal tribal and political languages; Turkic and other local languages were also present

gdpDescription.zh: 阿富汗经济以自给性农业、畜牧业和区域转运贸易为基础。山地地形限制大规模农业盈余，但其连接印度、中亚与伊朗的地理位置使通道税和商旅贸易具有重要价值。1840年因战争、军队掠夺和交通不稳，经济活动显著受扰
gdpDescription.en: The Afghan economy rested on subsistence agriculture, pastoralism, and regional transit trade. Mountainous terrain limited large agricultural surpluses, but the country’s position between India, Central Asia, and Iran gave customs and caravan trade considerable value. In 1840, warfare, military exactions, and insecure routes significantly disrupted economic life

mainIndustries.zh: 小麦与大麦种植、果园与葡萄干生产、畜牧与羊毛、马匹贸易、地毯与毛织品、皮革、地方金属加工、商队运输与关税征收
mainIndustries.en: Wheat and barley cultivation, orchards and raisin production, pastoralism and wool, horse trading, carpets and woolens, leatherwork, local metalworking, caravan transport, and customs collection

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与转运商品包括马匹、羊毛、干果、皮张、阿萨费提达胶、地毯；输入商品包括印度棉布、糖、香料、金属制品、军火与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exported and transit goods included horses, wool, dried fruits, hides, asafoetida, and carpets; imports included Indian cotton textiles, sugar, spices, metal goods, arms, and luxury wares

name.zh: 阿富汗卢比与提拉银币
name.en: Afghan rupee and tilla silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富主要以土地使用权、灌溉水权、牲畜、谷物储备、武器和家族网络体现，货币储蓄有限且分布不均
householdWealth.en: For most households, wealth was embodied chiefly in land-use rights, irrigation access, livestock, grain stores, arms, and kinship networks; monetary savings were limited and unevenly distributed

economicSystem.zh: 以传统农业和部族地方经济为基础的混合经济，国家对贸易征税但控制力有限，宫廷财政高度依赖关税、贡赋与外援
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on traditional agriculture and tribal local production, with the state taxing trade but exercising limited control; court finance depended heavily on customs, tribute, and external subsidy

source.en: Customs, transit tolls, and market dues

source.zh: 英国补贴与军事性资助
source.en: British subsidies and military support payments

source.zh: 其他罚金、官产与杂项收入
source.en: Other fines, crown domains, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 地方安抚、赠赐与外交支出
category.en: Provincial appeasement, gifts, and diplomatic expenses

category.zh: 宗教机构、法官与瓦克夫补助
category.en: Religious institutions, judges, and waqf support

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，现金储备不足以长期独立支撑战争。许多款项需迅速用于支付部族首领、亲军和宫廷开销，因此流动性脆弱，并部分依赖英方补贴维持
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in scale and lacked sufficient cash reserves to sustain prolonged war independently. Much of it had to be quickly spent on tribal chiefs, the royal guard, and court expenses, making liquidity fragile and partly dependent on British subsidy

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王位和短期军事稳定为目标，优先支付驻军、部族俸金和政治赏赐，而非建立持久性税制和官僚能力
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed chiefly at preserving the throne and short-term military stability, prioritizing garrisons, tribal stipends, and political patronage over building a durable tax system and bureaucratic capacity

description.zh: 由喀布尔政权直接供养的常备步兵，装备火绳枪、燧发枪和少量较新式枪械，训练与纪律参差不齐，部分受印度和英方军事影响
description.en: Standing infantry maintained directly by the Kabul regime, armed with matchlocks, flintlocks, and a small number of newer firearms; training and discipline were uneven, with some influence from Indian and British military practice

description.zh: 包括王室卫队、贵族骑兵和机动部队，适于突袭、护卫和联络，但持续后勤能力有限
description.en: Included the royal guard, noble cavalry, and mobile forces suited for raids, escort, and communications, though with limited sustained logistical capacity

description.zh: 由各部族在需要时动员的战士，是阿富汗战争力量的主体。其战斗意志强，熟悉山地，但服役短暂，服从性取决于酬金、荣誉与地方利益
description.en: Warriors mobilized by tribes when needed, forming the main body of Afghan armed strength. They possessed strong fighting spirit and mountain familiarity, but served briefly and obeyed according to pay, honor, and local interests

description.zh: 数量有限，火炮笨重且补给困难，多用于守城与震慑。与英印军相比技术、训练和机动性明显落后
description.en: Limited in number, with heavy guns difficult to supply; used mainly for siege defense and intimidation. Compared with the Anglo-Indian forces, their technology, training, and mobility lagged behind

commanderInChief.zh: 沙阿·舒贾·杜兰尼（名义上）；主要战地决策受英国将领与驻阿政治代表强烈影响
commanderInChief.en: Shah Shuja Durrani (nominally); major field decisions were strongly influenced by British generals and political officers in Afghanistan

technology.zh: 军事技术处于火药时代后期水平，主要依靠火绳枪、燧发枪、刀剑、长矛和少量火炮。山地作战经验丰富，但缺乏统一操典、工业化兵工生产和稳定补给体系
technology.en: Military technology stood at a late gunpowder-era level, relying chiefly on matchlocks, flintlocks, swords, spears, and a small artillery arm. Mountain warfare experience was extensive, but the kingdom lacked standardized drill, industrial arms production, and a stable supply system

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自巴拉克宰势力和多斯特·穆罕默德支持者的反抗、吉尔扎伊等部族动员、英国驻军与阿富汗社会间的冲突升级，以及波斯与中亚边境的不稳定因素
threats.en: Primary threats came from resistance by Barakzai forces and supporters of Dost Mohammad, mobilization by tribes such as the Ghilzai, escalating conflict between British garrisons and Afghan society, and instability along the Persian and Central Asian frontiers

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 线膛野战炮，使用开花弹
description.en: Rifled field gun firing explosive shells

description.zh: 柯尔特型转轮手枪，可连发六弹
description.en: Colt-type revolver capable of firing six rounds

description.zh: 套筒刺刀，仍为步兵白刃战标配
description.en: Socket bayonet, still standard for infantry close combat

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，分布极不均衡，集中于河谷绿洲、灌溉农业区和少数城市周边。主要族群包括普什图人、塔吉克人、哈扎拉人、乌兹别克人、奇兹尔巴什及其他山地与边地社群
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million and very unevenly distributed, concentrated in river valleys, irrigated agricultural zones, and the surroundings of a few towns. Major groups included Pashtuns, Tajiks, Hazaras, Uzbeks, Qizilbash, and other mountain and frontier communities

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、部族汗与地方首领、乌里玛和苏菲领袖、城镇商人与手工业者、农民、牧民以及依附性劳作者构成主要社会层次。阶层界限与族群、宗教和部族身份紧密交织
socialClasses.en: The principal social layers consisted of the royal house and nobility, tribal khans and local chiefs, ulama and Sufi leaders, urban merchants and artisans, peasants, pastoralists, and dependent laborers. Class boundaries were closely intertwined with ethnic, religious, and tribal identities

allies.zh: 主要外部支持者为英国东印度公司政权及其驻印体系；与部分亲杜兰尼地方首领保持脆弱合作
allies.en: The principal external supporter was the British East India Company regime and its Indian imperial system; the shah also maintained fragile cooperation with some pro-Durrani local chiefs

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为多斯特·穆罕默德及巴拉克宰派系、反英与反沙阿·舒贾的部族联盟；与波斯关系紧张，并警惕俄国在中亚方向的影响
enemies.en: The main hostile forces were Dost Mohammad and Barakzai factions, along with tribal coalitions opposed to both the British and Shah Shuja; relations with Persia were tense, and Russian influence from the Central Asian direction was watched with suspicion

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以依赖英国扶持保住王位为核心，同时试图通过赏赐、谈判和象征性宗主权重建对各地区的承认，但自主外交空间极其有限
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving the throne through British backing while trying to rebuild recognition in the provinces by gifts, negotiation, and symbolic overlordship; room for independent diplomacy was extremely limited

era.zh: 工业革命时代的边缘山地政体，整体仍以传统前工业技术为主
era.en: A peripheral mountain polity in the age of the Industrial Revolution, but overall still dominated by traditional pre-industrial technology

keyInnovations.zh: 无显著本土工业创新。较重要的技术变化主要来自输入式火器、改良炮械、部分印式军政组织方式，以及与英印交通和情报体系的接触
keyInnovations.en: There were no major indigenous industrial innovations. The most important technological changes came from imported firearms, improved artillery, some Indo-military administrative practices, and contact with Anglo-Indian transport and intelligence systems

strengths.zh: 地形险峻、部族战斗传统强、山地机动与游击能力突出；处于印度、中亚和伊朗之间，具有重要战略位置；宗教与地方忠诚可迅速动员抵抗外来势力
strengths.en: Its strengths were formidable terrain, strong tribal martial traditions, and excellent mountain mobility and irregular warfare capability. Positioned between India, Central Asia, and Iran, it held major strategic importance; religious and local loyalties could quickly mobilize resistance to foreign intervention

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权薄弱，财政基础狭窄，行政覆盖有限，王位合法性受质疑，并严重依赖英国军力与财政支持；部族割据和宗族竞争使全国难以长期统一动员
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were weak centralization, a narrow fiscal base, limited administrative reach, a disputed royal legitimacy, and heavy dependence on British military and financial support. Tribal fragmentation and dynastic rivalry made sustained nationwide mobilization difficult

outlook.zh: 1840年的阿富汗表面上完成王朝复辟，但政治秩序极不稳固。若英国驻军持续干预且无法赢得部族与城市精英支持，政权极可能爆发更大范围叛乱并迅速陷入崩溃
outlook.en: In 1840 Afghanistan appeared to have achieved a dynastic restoration, but the political order was highly unstable. If British occupation continued to interfere without securing tribal and urban elite support, the regime was likely to face wider rebellion and rapid collapse

description.zh: 第一次英阿战争期间的山地政权，中央权力受部族与外部干预挑战。
description.en: A mountain polity during the First Anglo-Afghan War, with central power challenged by tribes and foreign intervention.

ruler.zh: 拉金德拉·比克拉姆·沙阿

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，在强势贵族家族与宫廷派系影响下运作；1840年实际政务日益由首相及高阶贵族掌握
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy operating under the influence of powerful noble families and court factions; by 1840 practical administration was increasingly shaped by the prime minister and senior aristocracy

socialStructure.zh: 社会以印度教王权、山地贵族、军户与农民村社为核心，并叠加复杂的种姓、族群与地域等级；加德满都谷地的尼瓦尔城市社会与外部山地各族并存
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on Hindu kingship, hill aristocracy, military households, and peasant village communities, overlaid by complex hierarchies of caste, ethnicity, and region; the urban Newar society of the Kathmandu Valley coexisted with diverse hill peoples outside it

rulingClass.zh: 沙阿王室、塔帕与潘德等宫廷贵族家族、高种姓切特里与婆罗门官僚、军官集团以及加德满都谷地部分显贵
rulingClass.en: The Shah royal house, court noble families such as the Thapas and Pandes, high-caste Chhetri and Brahmin officials, the officer corps, and some leading elites of the Kathmandu Valley

succession.zh: 父系世袭，原则上由王室男性后裔继承，通常以嫡长子优先，但宫廷阴谋、后妃派系与贵族干预常影响实际继承与摄政安排
succession.en: Patrilineal hereditary succession within the royal house, in principle favoring the senior legitimate male heir, though palace intrigue, queenly factions, and aristocratic intervention often affected actual succession and regency arrangements

structure.zh: 中央政权以国王、首相（穆赫蒂亚尔）与宫廷议政机制为核心，配合军政合一的贵族官僚体系；地方由地区长官、税务承包者、堡垒守将与村社首领共同治理
structure.en: The central state was organized around the king, the mukhtiyar (chief minister/prime minister), and court deliberative mechanisms, supported by a noble bureaucracy with close military-administrative overlap; local rule combined regional governors, tax contractors, fort commanders, and village headmen

function.zh: 处理诏令、朝会、宫廷文书、王室事务与高层任命
function.en: Handled royal decrees, court audiences, palace documentation, royal household affairs, and high appointments

function.zh: 统筹全国政务、协调军政财政，并监督主要官员执行
function.en: Coordinated state administration, military and fiscal affairs, and supervised implementation by leading officials

function.zh: 管理地税、关卡税、盐税、土地登记、贡赋与仓储收入
function.en: Managed land tax, customs dues, salt revenue, land records, tribute obligations, and granary income

function.zh: 负责征兵、军饷、武器库、边防堡垒与部队调动
function.en: Oversaw recruitment, army pay, arsenals, frontier forts, and troop deployments

function.zh: 审理重大案件、解释法典、处理种姓与财产争议，并监督处罚执行
function.en: Tried major cases, interpreted legal codes, handled caste and property disputes, and supervised punishments

function.zh: 管理对英属印度与西藏方向的关卡、通行许可、贸易征税与边境情报
function.en: Managed checkpoints toward British India and Tibet, travel permits, trade taxation, and frontier intelligence

function.zh: 维护山道、桥梁、宫殿、寺庙、公仓与水利设施
function.en: Maintained mountain roads, bridges, palaces, temples, public granaries, and waterworks

function.zh: 管理王室祭祀、寺庙田产、节庆秩序及对婆罗门与佛教机构的资助
function.en: Managed royal ritual, temple lands, festival order, and patronage for Brahmin and Buddhist institutions

function.zh: 负责加德满都、帕坦、巴德岗等城市的市场监管、治安与工匠组织
function.en: Oversaw market regulation, policing, and artisan organization in Kathmandu, Patan, Bhaktapur, and other valley towns

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分层而不完全统一，核心谷地由王室与贵族直接控制，外部丘陵和塔赖地区则由巴达哈金、阿马利、苏巴及地方首领管理，辅以税农、堡寨守军和村社长老。国家对偏远山区的控制多依靠驻军、赋役网络与地方合作，而非高度文书化官僚统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was layered and not fully uniform. The core valley was under relatively direct royal and aristocratic control, while the outer hills and Tarai were managed by badahakim, amali, subba, and local chiefs, supported by revenue farmers, garrison posts, and village elders. In remote mountain regions, state control relied more on garrisons, tribute and labor networks, and local cooperation than on highly documentary bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以印度教王法、习惯法和王室敕令为基础。到1840年，统一成文法趋势已明显，并将在数年后以《穆卢基·艾因》形式进一步系统化。司法强调种姓秩序、土地权属、家族责任和宗教纯洁，刑罚包括罚金、没收、劳役、流放及体罚。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Hindu royal law, customary practice, and royal edicts. By 1840 there was already a clear movement toward legal codification, which would be systematized more fully a few years later in the Muluki Ain. Justice emphasized caste order, land rights, family responsibility, and ritual purity, with punishments including fines, confiscation, labor service, exile, and corporal penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入以土地税为主，辅以关税、盐税、市场税、林产与矿产收益、劳役义务、军户供给及部分贡赋。税收形式兼有实物与货币，山区多征粮、牲畜、纤维和劳役，谷地及贸易线则更常见银币缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue rested primarily on land taxation, supplemented by customs, salt revenue, market dues, forest and mineral income, corvée obligations, military household supply obligations, and some tribute. Collection was made in both kind and cash: hill areas often paid in grain, livestock, fiber, and labor, while the valley and trade corridors more often paid in silver coin.

religion.zh: 国家宗教与王权合法性以印度教为核心，尤其强调湿婆、毗湿奴与王室守护神崇拜；同时加德满都谷地保留深厚的尼瓦尔佛教传统，藏传佛教在北部山区亦具影响，民间信仰、祖先祭祀与地方神灵崇拜广泛存在。
religion.en: State religion and royal legitimacy were centered on Hinduism, especially the worship of Shiva, Vishnu, and royal tutelary deities; at the same time the Kathmandu Valley preserved a strong Newar Buddhist tradition, Tibetan Buddhism remained influential in the northern mountains, and popular religion included ancestor rites and widespread local deity worship.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想以印度教王权观、达摩秩序、等级义务和宗教合法性为基础；社会实践上则融合尼瓦尔城市商业伦理、藏喜马拉雅佛教宇宙观以及山地社群的习惯法传统。
philosophy.en: Political thought rested on Hindu ideas of kingship, dharma, hierarchical obligation, and religious legitimacy; in practice it was blended with Newar urban mercantile ethics, Tibetan-Himalayan Buddhist cosmologies, and the customary traditions of hill communities.

writingSystem.zh: 官方文书主要使用天城文书写尼泊尔语与梵语；加德满都谷地亦长期使用尼瓦尔文书写尼泊尔语（尼瓦尔语），宗教与跨喜马拉雅交流中可见兰札体和藏文字。
writingSystem.en: Official documents were written mainly in Devanagari for Nepali and Sanskrit; the Kathmandu Valley also long used scripts of the Newar tradition for Nepal Bhasa, while Ranjana and Tibetan scripts appeared in religious and trans-Himalayan contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 尼泊尔在1840年前后最突出的文化成就在于加德满都谷地高度成熟的寺庙与宫殿建筑、木雕与金属铸造、宗教节庆体系、尼瓦尔工艺传统以及融合印度教与佛教元素的城市文明。帕坦和巴德岗的铜像、塔庙、庭院与水泉系统尤具代表性。
culturalAchievements.en: Nepal's most notable cultural achievements around 1840 lay in the highly developed temple and palace architecture of the Kathmandu Valley, wood carving, metal casting, religious festival culture, Newar artisanal traditions, and an urban civilization that fused Hindu and Buddhist elements. The bronzes, pagoda temples, courtyards, and water systems of Patan and Bhaktapur were especially representative.

languageFamily.zh: 全国语言多样：宫廷与军政主导语言尼泊尔语属印欧语系印度-雅利安语族；尼瓦尔语属汉藏语系；北部与东部山区通行多种藏缅语支语言；宗教学术中梵语地位重要。
languageFamily.en: The kingdom was linguistically diverse: Nepali, the dominant court and military language, belonged to the Indo-European Indo-Aryan family; Newar belonged to the Sino-Tibetan family; many Tibeto-Burman languages were spoken in the northern and eastern hills; Sanskrit remained important in religion and scholarship.

unit.zh: 1840年尼泊尔莫哈尔银币购买力折算
1840年尼泊尔莫哈尔银币购买力折算

gdpDescription.zh: 尼泊尔经济以自给型山地农业为主，辅以谷地手工业、牲畜放牧、盐粮交换和跨喜马拉雅转口贸易。与英国统治下的印度平原相比，整体经济规模有限、商品化程度不高，但在山地环境中组织能力较强，并依靠战略位置维持稳定收入。
gdpDescription.en: Nepal's economy was primarily based on subsistence hill agriculture, supplemented by valley handicrafts, pastoralism, salt-for-grain exchange, and trans-Himalayan transit trade. Compared with the plains under British rule in India, the overall economy was modest and only partially commercialized, but it was relatively well organized for a mountain polity and sustained by its strategic location.

mainIndustries.zh: 梯田农业（稻、玉米、小麦、黍、荞麦）、畜牧、林产采集、金属工艺、铸币、小规模纺织、木雕、宗教器物制作、盐与羊毛转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Terraced agriculture (rice, maize, wheat, millet, buckwheat), animal husbandry, forest extraction, metalwork, minting, small-scale textiles, wood carving, manufacture of religious objects, and the transit trade in salt and wool

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与转运商品包括大米、酥油、牲畜、药材、木材、铜器、手工制品、藏盐、羊毛与麝香；输入商品包括棉布、金属制成品、奢侈品、香料、染料、部分武器与印度平原的日用品
tradeGoods.en: Exported and transit goods included rice, ghee, livestock, medicinal herbs, timber, copperware, handicrafts, Tibetan salt, wool, and musk; imports included cotton textiles, metal manufactures, luxury goods, spices, dyes, some weaponry, and everyday goods from the Indian plains

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富以土地使用权、谷物储备、牲畜、工具、宗教器物和家族劳动力形式存在，货币储蓄有限。谷地商人、贵族地主和税务承包者拥有相对可观的银币、珠宝与地产。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth existed in the form of land-use rights, grain stores, livestock, tools, ritual objects, and family labor, with limited cash savings. Valley merchants, noble landlords, and revenue contractors held more substantial stocks of silver coin, jewelry, and real estate.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社为基础、由国家地税和劳役制度支撑的前工业君主制经济；同时存在王室控制、贵族特权、寺庙土地、军户分配与民间市场交换并行的混合结构。
economicSystem.en: A preindustrial monarchical economy based on agrarian village communities and supported by state land taxation and labor obligations; it combined royal control, noble privilege, temple lands, military allotments, and private market exchange in a mixed structure.

source.en: Fines, judicial income, and miscellaneous levies

category.en: Court, royal household, and noble stipends

category.zh: 道路、桥梁、仓储与公共工程
category.en: Roads, bridges, granaries, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备由银币、贵金属器物、实物仓储和可迅速征调的地方资源构成。对山地国家而言，这一规模足以应付常规边防与宫廷开支，但面对长期大战或严重宫廷内乱时仍显脆弱。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver coin, precious metal objects, granary reserves in kind, and local resources that could be mobilized quickly. For a mountain state this was sufficient for ordinary frontier defense and court expenditure, but it remained vulnerable in the face of prolonged war or severe palace conflict.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体保守，优先维持边防、宫廷秩序与官军俸给，避免对农民征收过度破坏性现金税。国家重视控制贸易通道、维持盐与边贸收入，并在歉收时通过减免、调粮或劳役调整缓冲地方压力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, prioritizing frontier defense, court order, and the pay of officials and troops while avoiding excessively destructive cash demands on peasants. The state emphasized control of trade routes and the preservation of salt and customs income, and in poor harvest years it could ease pressure through remissions, grain transfers, or labor adjustments.

description.zh: 军队主体，由山地步兵、堡垒守军和地方征调部队构成，擅长崎岖地形行军、防御与近战，但火器训练和补给制度参差不齐。
description.en: The core of the armed forces, composed of hill infantry, fort garrisons, and locally levied troops, skilled in rough-terrain movement, defense, and close combat, though firearm training and supply systems were uneven.

description.zh: 拥有少量野战炮与城防炮，主要用于守城、山口防御和威慑。受限于山地运输条件，重炮机动性较差。
description.en: Included a limited number of field and fortress guns, used chiefly for siege defense, mountain-pass security, and deterrence. Heavy guns suffered from poor mobility in mountainous terrain.

description.zh: 驻守关键山口、塔赖据点与内部要塞，兼具治安、税卡和情报职责，是国家控制偏远地区的重要工具。
description.en: Stationed at key passes, Tarai posts, and internal strongholds, these troops performed security, customs, and intelligence duties and were essential for control over remote regions.

description.zh: 负责宫城安全、王室护卫和首都戒备，政治意义往往大于战场作用，并深受宫廷派系影响。
description.en: Responsible for palace security, royal protection, and guarding the capital; their political role often outweighed their battlefield value, and they were deeply affected by court factionalism.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王名义上为最高统帅，实权多由首相及主要军政贵族掌握
commanderInChief.en: The king was nominal commander-in-chief, though effective control often rested with the mukhtiyar and the principal military-aristocratic magnates

technology.zh: 军备处于前工业晚期水平，广泛使用燧发枪、火绳枪残余、刀剑、长枪和小型火炮。尼泊尔军队以山地作战经验著称，但在火器标准化、铸炮质量、兵站和操典方面落后于英属印度军队。
technology.en: Military technology was at a late preindustrial level, with widespread use of flintlocks, some remaining matchlocks, swords, spears, and small artillery. Nepalese forces were renowned for mountain warfare experience, but lagged behind the armies of British India in firearm standardization, artillery quality, logistics, and drill.

threats.zh: 主要外部威胁来自英属印度的政治与军事压力，尤其是条约体系下的不对称影响；北方与西藏—清帝国边境虽较稳定，但贸易、通道和边民纠纷仍可能引发摩擦。内部威胁则主要是宫廷政变、贵族派系冲突和地方控制力不足。
threats.en: The main external threat came from the political and military pressure of British India, especially the asymmetry created by treaty relations; the northern frontier with Tibet and the Qing sphere was relatively stable, but disputes over trade, transit, and border peoples could still cause friction. Internally, the chief dangers were palace coups, noble factional conflict, and weak control in some localities.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 漂浮或锚定水雷，封锁航道
description.en: Floating or anchored mine for blocking waterways

description.zh: 米尼弹线膛步枪，射程与精度大幅提升
description.en: Minié ball rifled musket with greatly improved range and accuracy

description.zh: 手摇式多管机枪，火力压制利器
description.en: Hand-cranked multi-barrel machine gun for fire suppression

populationDescription.zh: 1840年的尼泊尔人口约250万，主要分布在中部丘陵和加德满都谷地，塔赖平原人口较稀而受疟疾环境限制，北部高山地区则聚落分散。人口增长温和，受农业承载力、地形阻隔、疫病与迁徙模式影响明显。
populationDescription.en: Nepal's population in 1840 was about 2.5 million, concentrated mainly in the middle hills and the Kathmandu Valley; the Tarai remained more thinly populated due in part to malarial conditions, while the high Himalayan regions were sparsely settled. Population growth was moderate and strongly shaped by agricultural carrying capacity, terrain, disease, and migration patterns.

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括王室与宫廷贵族、高种姓婆罗门与切特里官僚军官、尼瓦尔商人与工匠、地方首领、寺庙与僧团附属人口、自由农民、佃农与役民，以及处于低种姓地位的服务群体。不同地区还存在古隆、马嘉尔、塔芒、赖、林布、塔卡利、塔鲁等族群，各自保有不同程度的自治传统与国家整合程度。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included the royal family and court nobility, high-caste Brahmin and Chhetri officials and officers, Newar merchants and artisans, local chiefs, populations attached to temples and monastic institutions, free peasants, tenants and laboring subjects, and service groups of lower caste status. Across different regions there were also Gurung, Magar, Tamang, Rai, Limbu, Thakali, Tharu, and other communities, each retaining varying degrees of autonomy and integration into the state.

allies.zh: 无正式强力盟友；与西藏保持以贸易和边境协商为基础的务实关系，并在对英关系上维持谨慎合作
allies.en: No major formal allies; Nepal maintained a pragmatic relationship with Tibet based on trade and frontier negotiation, while sustaining cautious cooperation in its dealings with the British

enemies.zh: 无公开全面战争中的正式敌国，但对英属印度保持深刻戒备；国内政治精英普遍警惕英国进一步干涉内政
enemies.en: No formal enemy in open full-scale war, but Nepal remained deeply wary of British India; the political elite generally feared further British interference in internal affairs

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交核心目标是维持独立、避免再度与英国发生灾难性战争、保持对西藏贸易通道的利益，并在清帝国、英国与周边喜马拉雅空间之间进行平衡。尼泊尔倾向有限开放、边境可控、重视礼仪和实用谈判的保守外交路线。
foreignPolicy.en: The core aim of foreign policy was to preserve independence, avoid another disastrous war with Britain, protect interests in trade routes to Tibet, and balance between the Qing sphere, the British, and the broader Himalayan world. Nepal favored a conservative diplomatic line of limited openness, controlled frontiers, and practical negotiation framed by formal protocol.

era.zh: 工业革命时代下的前工业山地国家
era.en: A preindustrial mountain state in the age of the Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 本国技术重点不在工业机械，而在适应山地环境的梯田农业、水渠系统、山路桥梁、木石建筑、铜铁工艺、铸币与宗教金属器制造。对外则逐步接触更先进的近代火器、钟表、测绘知识与部分英印制成品，但尚未形成本土工业化。
keyInnovations.en: Nepal's technological strengths did not lie in industrial machinery but in terrace agriculture adapted to mountain conditions, irrigation channels, mountain roads and bridges, timber-and-stone architecture, copper and iron crafts, minting, and the manufacture of religious metalwork. From abroad it was gradually exposed to more advanced modern firearms, clocks, surveying knowledge, and some manufactures from British India, but had not developed indigenous industrialization.

strengths.zh: 尼泊尔的优势在于险峻地形、坚韧的山地兵员、较强的王国认同、加德满都谷地成熟的手工业与财政基础，以及在英属印度与西藏之间的战略缓冲地位。国家虽不富裕，但具备在困难地形下维持统治和防御的能力。
strengths.en: Nepal's strengths lay in its rugged terrain, resilient mountain manpower, fairly strong sense of kingdom-wide identity, the mature artisanal and fiscal base of the Kathmandu Valley, and its strategic position as a buffer between British India and Tibet. Though not wealthy, the state was capable of sustaining rule and defense in difficult terrain.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点包括经济规模有限、交通成本高、地区整合不均、官僚制度不够统一、宫廷派系斗争激烈，以及在火器、训练和外交资源上远逊于英国。王权与贵族集团之间的紧张关系也削弱了国家长期稳定性。
weaknesses.en: Its chief weaknesses included a limited economic base, high transport costs, uneven regional integration, a not fully standardized bureaucracy, intense court factionalism, and inferiority to Britain in firearms, training, and diplomatic resources. Tension between monarchy and aristocratic power blocs also weakened long-term stability.

outlook.zh: 1840年的尼泊尔总体稳定且保持独立，但这种稳定更多依赖脆弱的宫廷平衡与对外谨慎，而非制度高度现代化。若贵族斗争升级，国家很可能出现权臣专政或宫廷暴力重组；若能维持内部秩序并继续与英国和西藏谨慎周旋，王国在中期内仍可保持独立存在。
outlook.en: In 1840 Nepal was broadly stable and independent, but this stability rested more on a fragile court balance and cautious external policy than on deeply modernized institutions. If aristocratic conflict intensified, the state was likely to experience domination by powerful ministers or violent palace reordering; if internal order held and diplomacy with Britain and Tibet remained careful, the kingdom could continue to preserve its independence in the medium term.

description.zh: 喜马拉雅山地王国，在英印与西藏之间保持独立。
description.en: A Himalayan mountain kingdom maintaining independence between British India and Tibet.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主专制为核心、依托宫廷、部族贵族、省级总督与宗教法学者协作运作的世袭伊朗君主制
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Iranian monarchy centered on royal absolutism, operating through the court, tribal aristocracy, provincial governors, and cooperation with the religious jurists.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与卡扎尔宗族成员、地方汗与部族首领、乌理玛与赛义德、城市商人与行会、农民、游牧部落及少数族群社群构成，地方差异显著。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal household and Qajar clan elites, provincial khans and tribal chiefs, ulama and sayyids, urban merchants and guilds, peasants, nomadic tribes, and minority communities, with strong regional variation.

rulingClass.zh: 卡扎尔王室、宫廷显贵、省级总督、部族领袖、高级军政官员以及与国家合作的什叶派宗教学者
rulingClass.en: The Qajar royal house, court grandees, provincial governors, tribal leaders, senior military-administrative officials, and Shi'i religious scholars who cooperated with the state.

succession.zh: 王位通常在卡扎尔王族内部世袭，由在位沙阿指定王储并争取宫廷、部族与宗教精英承认。
succession.en: The throne was ordinarily hereditary within the Qajar royal family, with the reigning shah designating a crown prince and securing recognition from court, tribal, and religious elites.

structure.zh: 1840年的波斯政府是高度人格化的宫廷国家。中央权力集中于沙阿及其近臣，但实际治理依赖首相、大臣、王室亲族、省总督、税务包收人和地方部族首领。中央机构尚未完全官僚化，许多职务随人而设，财政、军务与司法常彼此重叠。
structure.en: In 1840, the Persian government was a highly personalized court state. Central authority was concentrated in the shah and his intimates, but practical rule depended on the grand vizier, ministers, royal kinsmen, provincial governors, tax farmers, and local tribal chiefs. Central institutions were not yet fully bureaucratized, and many offices were personal rather than impersonal; fiscal, military, and judicial functions often overlapped.

function.zh: 统筹朝政，协调各部、地方总督与宫廷命令，是沙阿与行政体系之间的主要中介。
function.en: Coordinated affairs of state, supervised ministries, provincial governors, and court orders, and served as the chief intermediary between the shah and the administrative apparatus.

function.zh: 负责地税、关税、贡赋、包税契约、王室领地收入与国库支付。
function.en: Managed land tax, customs, tribute, tax-farming contracts, crown domain income, and treasury disbursements.

function.zh: 处理禁卫、地方驻军、部族征召、军饷、兵器和训练事务，并维持阿塞拜疆一带较为现代化的新军单位。
function.en: Handled the royal guard, provincial troops, tribal levies, military pay, armaments, and training, including the relatively more modernized units in Azerbaijan.

function.zh: 管理宫廷礼仪、王室地产、内廷供给、使节接待和赏赐体系。
function.en: Managed court ceremony, royal estates, palace provisioning, reception of envoys, and the patronage system.

function.zh: 依托沙里亚法官、城市穆智台希德和地方宗教机构处理婚姻、继承、契约与部分刑事案件。
function.en: Through sharia judges, urban mujtahids, and local religious institutions, it dealt with marriage, inheritance, contracts, and some criminal matters.

function.zh: 负责与俄国、英国、奥斯曼及周边政权的交涉、条约执行、边界争端和驻外使团事务。
function.en: Managed negotiations with Russia, Britain, the Ottoman Empire, and neighboring polities, including treaty implementation, border disputes, and diplomatic missions abroad.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省为主，由总督、王子总督或受命大臣治理，下辖城镇长官、税务承包人和部族首领。边疆地区如呼罗珊、阿塞拜疆、法尔斯和洛雷斯坦往往具有更强的地方自主性。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized mainly by provinces governed by governors, princely governors, or appointed ministers, under whom stood town officials, tax contractors, and tribal chiefs. Frontier regions such as Khorasan, Azerbaijan, Fars, and Luristan often enjoyed stronger local autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为什叶派伊斯兰法、王室敕令、地方习惯法与部族惯例并存。民事、家事和宗教事务主要由宗教法学者裁断，行政与治安案件则常由总督、军官或宫廷直接处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Twelver Shi'i Islamic law, royal decrees, local customary law, and tribal usage. Civil, familial, and religious matters were mainly adjudicated by religious jurists, while administrative and security cases were often handled directly by governors, officers, or the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税、农业贡赋、牧税、城市商税、关税和包税收入为主，征收效率受地方豪强、部族自治和财政外包影响较大，中央常难以足额收取应纳收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on land tax, agrarian dues, pastoral levies, urban commercial taxes, customs duties, and tax-farm revenues. Collection efficiency was heavily affected by local notables, tribal autonomy, and fiscal outsourcing, and the center often struggled to receive the full nominal yield.

religion.zh: 国教和社会主导宗教为十二伊玛目什叶派伊斯兰教，乌理玛网络在教育、司法、慈善和社会动员中影响深远；同时境内也有逊尼派、亚美尼亚使徒教会、亚述基督徒、犹太人、琐罗亚斯德教徒等群体。
religion.en: The state and socially dominant religion was Twelver Shi'i Islam, and the ulama network had deep influence in education, justice, charity, and social mobilization; at the same time, the realm also contained Sunni Muslims, Armenian Apostolics, Assyrian Christians, Jews, and Zoroastrians.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以什叶派法学、伊斯兰神学、波斯文学传统、伦理劝诫文和宫廷政治实用主义为核心；在高阶学术层面，伊斯法罕和其他中心仍保留萨德拉学派及其后的哲学影响。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Shi'i jurisprudence, Islamic theology, Persian literary tradition, ethical-admonitory writing, and courtly political pragmatism; at higher scholarly levels, Isfahan and other centers still preserved the influence of the Sadrian philosophical school and its successors.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为以阿拉伯字母书写的波斯文，行政、文学和宫廷通信皆以此为主；阿塞拜疆突厥语、阿拉伯语、亚美尼亚语和其他地方语言亦在各自社群中使用。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was Persian written in the Arabic script, used in administration, literature, and court correspondence; Azerbaijani Turkic, Arabic, Armenian, and other local languages were also used within their respective communities.

culturalAchievements.zh: 卡扎尔时期延续了波斯诗歌、书法、漆画、金工、地毯织造和宫廷肖像画传统。德黑兰、伊斯法罕、设拉子和大不里士是文化中心；建筑上重视清真寺、宗教学校、宫苑和城市门楼，视觉艺术中出现较鲜明的王室肖像风格。
culturalAchievements.en: The Qajar period continued the Persian traditions of poetry, calligraphy, lacquerwork, metalwork, carpet weaving, and court portraiture. Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, and Tabriz were cultural centers; in architecture, mosques, madrasas, palace gardens, and urban gateways remained important, while the visual arts developed a distinctive royal portrait style.

languageFamily.zh: 主导语言属印欧语系伊朗语支，以波斯语为国家通用行政文化语言；境内另有突厥语族、闪米特语族、亚美尼亚语和高加索诸语使用者。
languageFamily.en: The dominant language belonged to the Iranian branch of the Indo-European family, with Persian serving as the principal administrative and cultural language; the realm also included speakers of Turkic, Semitic, Armenian, and various Caucasian languages.

gdpDescription.zh: 波斯经济在1840年仍以农业和牧业为基础，城市手工业和区域贸易为补充。与西欧工业化国家相比，总体生产率较低，但在西亚仍属有分量的大型经济体，尤其依托地理位置连接高加索、两河、印度洋与中亚贸易路线。
gdpDescription.en: In 1840, the Persian economy remained fundamentally agrarian and pastoral, supplemented by urban crafts and regional trade. Compared with the industrializing states of Western Europe, overall productivity was low, but within West Asia it remained a substantial large economy, benefiting especially from its position linking the Caucasus, Mesopotamia, the Indian Ocean, and Central Asian trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、小麦与大麦、园艺作物、棉花、丝绸、地毯、畜牧、皮革、染织、金属手工业、驮运贸易、海湾港口商业
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wheat and barley, horticultural crops, cotton, silk, carpets, livestock raising, leatherworking, dyeing and textiles, metal crafts, caravan trade, and Gulf port commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以生丝、地毯、棉花、鸦片、干果、皮张、马匹和部分手工业品为主；进口以棉纺织品、金属制品、武器、糖、茶、咖啡和奢侈品为主。
tradeGoods.en: Exports chiefly included raw silk, carpets, cotton, opium, dried fruits, hides, horses, and some manufactured crafts; imports included cotton textiles, metal goods, arms, sugar, tea, coffee, and luxury items.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富高度不均。城市商人、放贷者、宗教地产管理者和地方显贵积累了可观资产，而广大农民和游牧民多处于自给与低现金化状态，易受歉收、征敛和地方冲突影响。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Urban merchants, moneylenders, custodians of religious endowments, and local grandees accumulated significant assets, while most peasants and nomads lived in largely subsistence and low-cash conditions, vulnerable to harvest failure, exactions, and local conflict.

unit.en: international dollars (1990 benchmark) per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为传统农业—商贸混合体系，国家直接控制能力有限，王室领地、私人地产、宗教捐产、部族放牧区和包税网络并存，市场交换活跃但区域分割明显。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a traditional agrarian-commercial mixed order with limited direct state control, in which crown lands, private estates, religious endowments, tribal pasture zones, and tax-farming networks coexisted; market exchange was active, but regional fragmentation remained pronounced.

source.zh: 包税、城市商税与行会课征
source.en: Tax farming, urban commercial taxes, and guild levies

category.zh: 公共工程、宗教捐助与杂费
category.en: Public works, religious support, and miscellaneous costs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，现金流高度依赖年度征收、预支和地方转运。名义账面收入与实际可支配现银之间常有差距，沙阿往往需要依靠临时征敛、包税预付款和宫廷调拨维持稳定。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in scale, and cash flow depended heavily on annual collections, advances, and transfers from the provinces. There was often a gap between nominal book revenue and actually disposable specie, so the shah frequently relied on extraordinary exactions, tax-farm advances, and court reallocations to preserve stability.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，以维持宫廷、军队和地方忠诚为优先，重视关税与传统赋税而非结构性改革。政府意在保持收支平衡，但执行受腐败、地方扣留和征收成本制约。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, prioritizing the maintenance of the court, the army, and provincial loyalty, and relying on customs and traditional imposts rather than structural reform. The government aimed to preserve budgetary balance, but implementation was constrained by corruption, local retention, and the cost of collection.

description.zh: 负责首都与王室安全，装备和待遇通常优于一般部队，是政权稳定的重要支柱。
description.en: Responsible for the security of the capital and the royal household, these troops were generally better equipped and paid than ordinary forces and formed an important pillar of regime stability.

description.zh: 包括受欧式训练影响的新式步兵单位，主要驻扎于阿塞拜疆、德黑兰及战略要地，但训练与补给仍不均衡。
description.en: Included newer infantry units influenced by European drill, mainly stationed in Azerbaijan, Tehran, and strategic points, though training and supply remained uneven.

description.zh: 以部族征召和地方封臣骑兵为主，机动性强，适合边疆巡防和快速集结，但纪律、统一指挥和持久作战能力有限。
description.en: Composed mainly of tribal levies and provincial cavalry retainers, they were highly mobile and useful for frontier patrol and rapid concentration, but limited in discipline, unified command, and sustained campaigning ability.

description.zh: 拥有一定数量火炮与炮手，部分受俄国和欧洲技术影响，但制造、运输、火药供应和瞄准训练仍落后于列强。
description.en: Maintained a notable number of guns and gunners, some influenced by Russian and broader European techniques, but manufacturing, transport, powder supply, and gunnery training still lagged behind the great powers.

name.zh: 地方守备与民兵预备队

description.zh: 分布于各省城镇、要塞和边区，在战时补充常备军，平时承担治安、护路和守城任务。
description.en: Distributed across provincial towns, forts, and border zones, they reinforced the standing army in wartime and in peacetime handled policing, road security, and local defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 穆罕默德·沙阿为名义最高统帅，实际统军多经由王子、省总督与高级将领分掌
commanderInChief.en: Mohammad Shah was the nominal commander-in-chief, while actual military control was often exercised through princes, provincial governors, and senior generals.

technology.zh: 军队技术处于前近代向近代过渡阶段，广泛使用火绳枪、燧发枪、冷兵器和旧式火炮，少数部队接受欧式操练。后勤、军工和标准化水平明显弱于俄国与英国印度军。
technology.en: Military technology stood in a transitional stage between early modern and modern systems, with widespread use of matchlocks, flintlocks, edged weapons, and older artillery, while some units received European-style drill. Logistics, military industry, and standardization were distinctly weaker than those of Russia and the British Indian forces.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方俄国的持续南压、东方赫拉特与阿富汗方向的不稳、奥斯曼边境摩擦，以及英国通过印度洋与海湾事务施加的外交压力。
threats.en: Principal threats came from continued Russian pressure from the north, instability in the Herat and Afghan direction to the east, friction along the Ottoman frontier, and diplomatic pressure exerted by Britain through Indian Ocean and Gulf affairs.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 克虏伯式后装线膛钢炮
description.en: Krupp-style breech-loading rifled steel cannon

description.zh: 漂浮或锚定水雷，封锁航道
description.en: Floating or anchored mine for blocking waterways

description.zh: 米尼弹线膛步枪，射程与精度大幅提升
description.en: Minié ball rifled musket with greatly improved range and accuracy

populationDescription.zh: 1840年波斯人口约一千万，分布不均，集中于高原农业区、绿洲城市和西北部较富庶地区。人口增长缓慢，受旱灾、疫病、部族迁徙和地方战争影响明显。
populationDescription.en: In 1840 Persia had a population of about 10 million, unevenly distributed and concentrated in plateau farming zones, oasis cities, and the more prosperous northwest. Population growth was slow and significantly affected by drought, disease, tribal migration, and local warfare.

description.zh: 萨法维王朝首都，世界之半
description.en: Safavid capital, 'half the world'

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括王室与部族贵族、宗教学者、军政官员、城市商人与手工业者、农民、佃户、游牧部落成员及少数族群共同体。许多地区的部族身份与定居农业社会并行，是国家统治的重要结构特征。
socialClasses.en: The principal social classes included the royal family and tribal nobility, religious scholars, military-administrative officials, urban merchants and artisans, peasants, tenants, nomadic tribal populations, and minority communities. In many regions, tribal identity coexisted with settled agrarian society and remained a key structural feature of state rule.

allies.zh: 波斯在1840年缺乏稳定而可靠的正式盟友，更多采取在俄国与英国之间周旋的平衡外交；与部分阿拉伯海湾地方势力及若干边疆部族存在临时合作。
allies.en: Persia lacked stable and reliable formal allies in 1840, and instead pursued balancing diplomacy between Russia and Britain; it also maintained temporary cooperation with some Gulf Arab powers and various frontier tribes.

enemies.zh: 最具战略压力的对手是俄国；与英国虽非公开战争状态，但在赫拉特、阿富汗和海湾问题上存在尖锐利益冲突；与奥斯曼帝国亦有边界争议。
enemies.en: Its most strategically pressing adversary was Russia; although not in open war with Britain, there were sharp conflicts of interest over Herat, Afghanistan, and the Gulf; Persia also had boundary disputes with the Ottoman Empire.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护领土完整、恢复对呼罗珊及赫拉特方向的影响、限制俄英干涉并保持王朝尊严为核心。外交方式以使节谈判、边疆军事威慑、朝贡关系与宫廷网络相结合。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving territorial integrity, restoring influence toward Khorasan and Herat, limiting Russian and British interference, and maintaining dynastic prestige. It combined diplomatic negotiation, frontier military pressure, tributary expectations, and courtly networks.

era.zh: 工业革命时代下的前工业—早期近代过渡阶段
era.en: A transitional pre-industrial to early modern stage within the age of the Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 波斯本身尚未工业化，但已有限接触欧式军训、铸炮技术、改良火器、外交文书制度和部分新式制造观念。交通仍主要依赖驮运、商队与沿海帆船，通讯靠驿站和信使。
keyInnovations.en: Persia itself was not yet industrialized, but had limited contact with European military drill, artillery casting techniques, improved firearms, diplomatic paperwork systems, and some newer manufacturing ideas. Transport still relied mainly on pack animals, caravans, and coastal sailing craft, while communications depended on relay posts and couriers.

strengths.zh: 波斯拥有深厚的王朝合法性、共同的什叶派宗教认同、连接多区域贸易路线的地理优势，以及在阿塞拜疆、法尔斯和呼罗珊等地仍具动员能力的地方精英网络。
strengths.en: Persia possessed deep dynastic legitimacy, a shared Shi'i religious identity, a geographic advantage linking multiple trade regions, and local elite networks in areas such as Azerbaijan, Fars, and Khorasan that still retained mobilizing power.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于财政基础薄弱、行政官僚化不足、军队训练和装备不均、地方主义和部族自治强，以及面对俄英时外交与军事资源明显不足。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a fragile fiscal base, insufficient bureaucratic centralization, uneven military training and equipment, strong localism and tribal autonomy, and clearly inadequate diplomatic and military resources when confronting Russia and Britain.

outlook.zh: 就1840年而言，波斯局势总体稳定，但这种稳定带有脆弱性。若王朝无法推进财政、军政和省级控制改革，未来数十年将更易在列强压力下被迫让步；若能借贸易、有限军改和行政整顿增强中央能力，则仍可维持区域性国家地位。
outlook.en: As of 1840, Persia was broadly stable, but that stability was fragile. If the dynasty failed to advance fiscal, military-administrative, and provincial control reforms, it would become increasingly vulnerable to great-power pressure in the decades ahead; if it could strengthen the center through trade, limited military reform, and administrative consolidation, it could still preserve its position as a regional state.

description.zh: 卡扎尔王朝统治下的伊朗国家，在俄英压力间寻求维持区域地位。
description.en: An Iranian state under the Qajar dynasty seeking to preserve its regional position amid Russian and British pressure.

name.zh: 穆罕默德·阿里治下的埃及

governmentForm.zh: 名义上隶属奥斯曼帝国、实际上高度自治的军事官僚君主制
governmentForm.en: A military-bureaucratic monarchy nominally under the Ottoman Empire but effectively highly autonomous

socialStructure.zh: 社会以农村农民为主体，上层由王朝家族、土耳其语与阿尔巴尼亚裔军政精英、官僚与大地主构成；城市中有乌里玛、商人、手工业者与行会成员，另有大量被征召的农民士兵与国家工场劳工。
socialStructure.en: Society was predominantly rural and peasant-based, with an upper stratum of the ruling household, Turkish- and Albanian-speaking military-administrative elites, bureaucrats, and large landholders; urban society included ulama, merchants, artisans, and guild members, alongside large numbers of conscripted peasant soldiers and state workshop laborers.

rulingClass.zh: 穆罕默德·阿里家族、帕夏亲信军官团、中央官僚、税务与省政长官
rulingClass.en: The household of Muhammad Ali, his loyal officer corps, central bureaucrats, and provincial and fiscal administrators

succession.zh: 事实上实行家族世袭，由穆罕默德·阿里指定继承人，但仍需奥斯曼苏丹正式承认
succession.en: De facto dynastic succession within Muhammad Ali's family, with the ruler designating an heir, though formal recognition by the Ottoman sultan remained necessary

structure.zh: 政权高度中央集权，核心围绕总督本人、迪万体系、军队指挥机构与财政垄断机关运作。埃及本土及其所控制的叙利亚、苏丹部分地区由省级官员、军政长官与税务代理共同管理。
structure.en: The regime was highly centralized, operating around the wali himself, the divan system, military command structures, and fiscal monopoly agencies. Egypt proper and its controlled territories in Syria and parts of Sudan were administered through provincial officials, military governors, and tax agents.

function.zh: 负责土地税、国家垄断收入、预算编制、薪饷发放与征收监管
function.en: Managed land tax, state monopoly revenues, budget preparation, payroll, and collection oversight

function.zh: 负责征兵、军队编制、训练、军械与驻防调度
function.en: Oversaw conscription, army organization, training, armaments, and garrison deployment

function.zh: 管理亚历山大海军设施、造船、港口军务与海上运输
function.en: Administered the naval installations at Alexandria, shipbuilding, port military affairs, and maritime transport

name.en: Diwan of Interior and Provincial Affairs

function.zh: 负责地方行政、治安、人口登记、劳役征发与省长监督
function.en: Handled local administration, policing, population registration, corvée mobilization, and supervision of provincial governors

function.zh: 统制棉花、粮食及其他关键商品的收购、定价与出口
function.en: Controlled the procurement, pricing, and export of cotton, grain, and other key commodities

name.en: Bureau of Education and Medical Affairs

function.zh: 管理新式学校、翻译机构、技术留学与军医体系
function.en: Managed modern schools, translation bureaus, technical missions abroad, and the military medical system

localAdmin.zh: 地方上由省长、县级官员、村长与税务代理共同治理；中央通过人口调查、灌溉监督、征兵与税收网络深入乡村。叙利亚等新占领地区更多依赖军政总督和驻军控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted through provincial governors, district officials, village headmen, and tax agents; the center penetrated the countryside through census-taking, irrigation oversight, conscription, and tax networks. Newly occupied regions such as Syria relied more heavily on military governors and garrisons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为奥斯曼伊斯兰法、苏丹敕令与总督行政命令并行。宗教法院继续处理婚姻、继承等事务，而军事、财政与行政领域 increasingly 由国家规章和官僚命令主导。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Ottoman Islamic law, sultanic decrees, and the wali's administrative ordinances. Religious courts continued to handle marriage, inheritance, and related matters, while military, fiscal, and administrative affairs were increasingly governed by state regulations and bureaucratic orders.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以土地税、人头与附加征发、国家垄断利润、关税和征发劳役为基础。政府通过直接收购与统一转售控制农产品收入，尤其依赖长绒棉出口。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on land taxes, capitation and supplementary levies, state monopoly profits, customs duties, and labor exactions. The government controlled agrarian income through direct procurement and centralized resale, relying especially on long-staple cotton exports.

religion.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰教为主，科普特基督徒、犹太社群及其他少数宗教群体在城市与地方社会中持续存在。阿兹哈尔仍是宗教权威中心之一，但国家对宗教机构的约束较前增强。
religion.en: Sunni Islam predominated, while Coptic Christians, Jewish communities, and other minority groups remained present in urban and local society. Al-Azhar continued as a major center of religious authority, though the state exercised stronger control over religious institutions than before.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想以伊斯兰君主统治、奥斯曼行政传统与务实的国家富强观相结合，强调军事改革、技术学习和国家监督经济。知识界开始接触欧洲科学、医学与工程思想，但尚未形成成熟的自由主义政治文化。
philosophy.en: Political thought blended Islamic rulership, Ottoman administrative tradition, and a pragmatic vision of state strengthening, emphasizing military reform, technical learning, and state supervision of the economy. Intellectuals were beginning to engage with European science, medicine, and engineering, but a mature liberal political culture had not yet emerged.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语；行政和宫廷环境中亦可见奥斯曼土耳其语文书。新式学校推动翻译、技术术语与印刷出版的扩展。
writingSystem.en: Arabic was written primarily in the Arabic script; Ottoman Turkish documents also appeared in administrative and courtly settings. Modern schools encouraged translation, technical vocabulary, and expanded printing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的重要成就在于国家主导的现代化文化设施建设，包括布拉格印刷所的发展、翻译运动、新式军校与医学校、工程教育以及地图测绘。传统伊斯兰学术与新式官学并存，形成过渡性文化格局。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements of the period lay in state-sponsored modernizing institutions, including the development of the Bulaq Press, translation movements, modern military and medical schools, engineering education, and cartography. Traditional Islamic scholarship and new official education coexisted in a transitional cultural order.

languageFamily.zh: 阿非罗-亚细亚语系，以阿拉伯语为主；行政上兼有突厥语影响
languageFamily.en: Afro-Asiatic language family, predominantly Arabic; administration also showed Turkic influence

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年前后的埃及经济在中东地区属较强水平，依托尼罗河农业、国家垄断体系、棉花出口和国家工场发展，但其扩张建立在高压征收、征兵与强制劳动基础上，且战争开支沉重。
gdpDescription.en: By around 1840, Egypt's economy ranked among the stronger ones in the Middle East, resting on Nile agriculture, state monopolies, cotton exports, and state manufactories; however, its expansion was built on heavy extraction, conscription, and forced labor, and it was burdened by substantial war expenditures.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、长绒棉种植、粮食生产、制糖、纺织、军需制造、造船、火药与兵工、运输与仓储
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, long-staple cotton cultivation, grain production, sugar processing, textiles, military supply manufacturing, shipbuilding, gunpowder and armaments, transport, and storage

tradeGoods.zh: 棉花、稻米、小麦、亚麻、糖、阿拉伯胶、象牙、咖啡转口、军需物资、木材与金属制品进口
tradeGoods.en: Cotton, rice, wheat, flax, sugar, gum arabic, ivory, re-exported coffee, military supplies, and imported timber and metal goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富有限，多以土地使用、牲畜、粮食储备和家庭劳动力为主要资产；城市商人与高官积累了更明显的货币财富与不动产。国家对土地与作物的强力控制压缩了民间资本形成。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary peasant households possessed limited wealth, chiefly in land use rights, livestock, grain stores, and family labor; urban merchants and high officials accumulated more visible monetary wealth and real estate. Strong state control over land and crops constrained private capital formation.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的垄断经济与军事财政体制，核心部门由官营机构控制，农民在行政命令和固定收购体系下生产。该体制促进了快速动员资源，但效率低下且社会成本很高。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed monopoly economy and military-fiscal system, with key sectors controlled by government agencies and peasants producing under administrative orders and fixed procurement systems. This system enabled rapid resource mobilization but was inefficient and socially costly.

source.zh: 贡赋、附加税与杂项收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍具相当动员能力，但因叙利亚战争、海军建设和欧洲列强施压而承受巨大负担。账面资源不少以仓储物资、垄断库存和待征收入形式存在，现金流并不宽裕。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still possessed substantial mobilization capacity, but it was under severe strain from the Syrian war, naval construction, and pressure from European powers. Much of its apparent wealth existed in stored goods, monopoly stocks, and expected revenues rather than abundant liquid cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以最大化国家征收和维持军队为优先，依赖高税负、垄断经营与行政强制。短期内有利于军政扩张，长期则造成农村负担沉重、走私增加与生产积极性受损。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maximizing state extraction and maintaining the armed forces, relying on heavy taxation, monopolies, and administrative coercion. This supported military and administrative expansion in the short term, but in the longer term it imposed severe rural burdens, encouraged smuggling, and undermined productive incentives.

description.zh: 以征募农民和长期训练的新式步兵为核心，按欧洲操典组织，装备燧发枪和野战炮支援，是埃及军力的主体。
description.en: The core arm consisted of conscripted peasants and long-trained modern infantry, organized along European drill principles and equipped with flintlock muskets and field artillery support.

description.zh: 包括常备骑兵和部分不规则部队，用于侦察、追击、联络和边疆行动，在阿拉伯与叙利亚战场尤其重要。
description.en: Included regular cavalry and some irregular formations, used for reconnaissance, pursuit, communications, and frontier operations, especially important in Arabian and Syrian campaigns.

description.zh: 炮兵是改革重点之一，拥有攻城炮、野战炮和训练有素的炮兵团，在对奥斯曼作战中表现突出。
description.en: Artillery was one of the chief focuses of reform, including siege guns, field artillery, and well-trained artillery regiments that performed notably in wars against the Ottomans.

description.zh: 以亚历山大为基地，拥有战舰、运输船和海军工场，但在1839至1840年危机中受制于列强干预，难与英国皇家海军抗衡。
description.en: Based at Alexandria, the navy possessed warships, transports, and naval workshops, but in the 1839-1840 crisis it was constrained by great-power intervention and could not match the Royal Navy.

description.zh: 负责要塞、交通线、仓储、渡河与补给，支撑埃及跨区域军事行动。
description.en: Handled fortresses, lines of communication, storage, river crossing, and supply, supporting Egypt's transregional military operations.

technology.zh: 军队采用欧洲式编制、操练与制服，广泛使用燧发枪、野战炮、攻城炮、军医制度和兵工厂制造。虽具地区领先水平，但后勤、海军技术与工业基础仍落后于英法等强国。
technology.en: The military used European-style organization, drill, and uniforms, with widespread use of flintlock muskets, field and siege artillery, military medicine, and arsenal production. It was regionally advanced, though its logistics, naval technology, and industrial base still lagged behind powers such as Britain and France.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自奥斯曼帝国、英国海军干预以及1840年东方危机中欧洲列强的联合施压；叙利亚与阿拉伯占领区也存在地方反抗和补给困难。
threats.en: The principal threats came from the Ottoman Empire, British naval intervention, and the combined pressure of European powers during the 1840 Eastern Crisis; local resistance and logistical difficulties also afflicted the occupied regions of Syria and Arabia.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 大口径攻城臼炮与加农炮
description.en: Large-caliber siege mortars and cannons

description.zh: 蒸汽动力铁甲战船，木帆船终结者
description.en: Steam-powered ironclad warship, rendering wooden ships obsolete

description.zh: 米尼弹线膛步枪，射程与精度大幅提升
description.en: Minié ball rifled musket with greatly improved range and accuracy

description.zh: 柯尔特型转轮手枪，可连发六弹
description.en: Colt-type revolver capable of firing six rounds

description.zh: 手摇式多管机枪，火力压制利器
description.en: Hand-cranked multi-barrel machine gun for fire suppression

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于尼罗河三角洲和河谷，农村人口占绝大多数。开罗和亚历山大是最大的城市中心，叙利亚等控制区的人口未完全计入埃及本土总数。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Nile Delta and Valley, with an overwhelming rural majority. Cairo and Alexandria were the largest urban centers, while the populations of controlled regions such as Syria were not fully counted within the Egyptian core total.

description.zh: 法蒂玛/马穆鲁克首都，伊斯兰世界中心
description.en: Fatimid-Mamluk capital, center of the Islamic world

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王朝统治家族与军政贵显、乌里玛与宗教人士、城市商人和手工业者、科普特文书与财政人员、普通市民、广大的农民阶层，以及军队中的征召士兵与服劳役者。奴隶仍存在于家内服务、军政网络和上层家庭之中，但不构成整体经济主体。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the ruling dynasty and military-administrative grandees, ulama and religious figures, urban merchants and artisans, Coptic clerks and fiscal personnel, ordinary townspeople, the broad peasant mass, and conscript soldiers and corvée laborers. Slavery still existed in domestic service, military-administrative networks, and elite households, though it was not the main basis of the overall economy.

allies.zh: 法国在军事技术与顾问层面长期具有重要影响，但到1840年并未给予决定性军事援助；埃及在阿拉伯和苏丹部分地区有附属或受控势力。
allies.en: France retained long-standing importance in military technique and advisory influence, though by 1840 it did not provide decisive military aid; Egypt also possessed subordinate or controlled forces in parts of Arabia and Sudan.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对方为奥斯曼帝国；在东方危机中，英国、奥地利和俄国等列强支持恢复奥斯曼权威，使埃及面临国际孤立和海上封锁风险。
enemies.en: The chief enemy was the Ottoman Empire; during the Eastern Crisis, powers such as Britain, Austria, and Russia supported the restoration of Ottoman authority, leaving Egypt internationally isolated and vulnerable to naval blockade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策旨在通过军事扩张和谈判确认对埃及、苏丹、叙利亚及阿拉伯部分地区的控制，并争取王朝世袭地位。1840年时，该战略已因列强干预而接近极限，被迫在保全埃及世袭统治与放弃叙利亚之间权衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy sought to secure Egyptian, Sudanese, Syrian, and Arabian possessions through military expansion and diplomacy, while obtaining hereditary status for the dynasty. By 1840 this strategy had reached its limits under great-power intervention, forcing a choice between preserving hereditary rule in Egypt and retaining Syria.

era.zh: 工业革命时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键进展包括新式兵工厂、布拉格印刷所、现代医学教育、工程与测绘学校、蒸汽动力有限引入、改良灌溉工程以及按欧洲模式训练的军队与官僚体系。埃及虽未完成工业化，但已成为近代化改革最积极的伊斯兰国家之一。
keyInnovations.en: Key advances included modern arsenals, the Bulaq Press, modern medical education, engineering and surveying schools, limited introduction of steam power, improved irrigation works, and armies and bureaucracies trained on European models. Egypt had not industrialized fully, but it had become one of the most active reforming Islamic states.

strengths.zh: 国家动员能力强，军队改革成效显著，农业与棉花出口带来可观收入，中央集权与新式教育提升了行政效率，在中东地区具有突出的军事和制度优势。
strengths.en: The state had strong mobilization capacity, successful military reforms, substantial income from agriculture and cotton exports, and improved administrative efficiency through centralization and modern education, giving it notable military and institutional advantages in the Middle East.

weaknesses.zh: 体制过度依赖个人统治、高压征收与战争扩张；农村社会负担沉重，经济对国家垄断和强制劳动依赖过深，财政赤字明显，海军与工业仍不足以对抗欧洲列强。
weaknesses.en: The system depended excessively on personal rule, coercive extraction, and military expansion; the countryside was heavily burdened, the economy relied too deeply on monopolies and forced labor, fiscal deficits were pronounced, and the navy and industry remained insufficient against European great powers.

outlook.zh: 1840年埃及正处于盛极而衰的转折点。若接受列强安排并保住埃及的世袭统治，王朝仍可继续作为区域强国存在；但其跨帝国扩张事业，尤其对叙利亚的控制，已难以长期维持。
outlook.en: In 1840 Egypt stood at a turning point from peak expansion toward retrenchment. If it accepted the great powers' settlement and preserved hereditary rule in Egypt, the dynasty could continue as a regional power; however, its project of trans-imperial expansion, especially its hold over Syria, was no longer sustainable in the long run.

description.zh: 名义上属奥斯曼帝国、实际上高度自主的军政国家，曾向叙利亚和阿拉伯扩张。
description.en: Nominally Ottoman but effectively autonomous, this military-bureaucratic state had expanded into Syria and Arabia.

governmentForm.zh: 伊斯兰君主制下的中央集权帝国，结合宫廷官僚、行省总督制与宗教法统治
governmentForm.en: A centralized Islamic imperial monarchy combining palace bureaucracy, provincial governorships, and rule through religious law

socialStructure.zh: 社会大体由王朝与宫廷精英、乌里玛与宗教阶层、军政官僚、省级显贵与地主、城市行会商人、农民、部族人口以及帝国内各米利特宗教共同体构成
socialStructure.en: Society was broadly composed of the dynasty and palace elite, the ulema and religious establishment, military-administrative officials, provincial notables and landlords, urban guild merchants, peasants, tribal populations, and the empire’s various millet religious communities

rulingClass.zh: 苏丹、宫廷高官、帕夏、乌里玛上层与受朝廷承认的省级显贵共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the sultan, high palace officials, pashas, the upper ulema, and provincially entrenched notables recognized by the imperial center

succession.zh: 奥斯曼王朝世袭继承，通常由王朝男性成员承继苏丹位，经宫廷与官僚体系承认
succession.en: Hereditary succession within the Ottoman dynasty, usually passing to a male dynastic member and confirmed by the palace and bureaucratic establishment

structure.zh: 1840年的帝国政府正处于从传统宫廷政务向改革型中央官僚制过渡的阶段。苏丹居于最高权力核心，大维齐尔统领日常政务，中央通过枢密体系、财政机关、司法宗教机构和各省总督实施统治；同时仍受地方显贵、埃及危机与边疆军事压力制约
structure.en: In 1840 the imperial government was in transition from traditional palace-centered administration toward a reforming centralized bureaucracy. The sultan stood at the apex, the grand vizier directed day-to-day government, and the center ruled through imperial councils, fiscal offices, judicial-religious institutions, and provincial governors; yet it remained constrained by local notables, the Egyptian crisis, and frontier military pressures

function.zh: 统筹帝国政务、协调各部、向苏丹呈报军政财政事务
function.en: Coordinated imperial administration, supervised major offices, and reported military, political, and fiscal matters to the sultan

function.zh: 征收税赋、编制预算、管理国债与国库支付
function.en: Collected taxes, prepared budgets, and managed public debt obligations and treasury payments

function.zh: 起草敕令、管理档案、监督地方行政与公文流转
function.en: Drafted decrees, maintained archives, and oversaw provincial administration and official correspondence

name.zh: 谢赫伊斯兰与宗教司法机构
name.en: Office of the Sheikh al-Islam and Religious Judiciary

function.zh: 管理伊斯兰法解释、任命法官、监督宗教教育与法庭体系
function.en: Administered interpretation of Islamic law, appointed judges, and supervised religious education and the court system

function.zh: 管理常备军、军需、炮兵、要塞与征募事务
function.en: Managed the standing army, logistics, artillery, fortresses, and recruitment

function.zh: 负责舰队、造船厂、海峡防务与海军补给
function.en: Oversaw the fleet, dockyards, straits defense, and naval supply

localAdmin.zh: 地方上以省、桑贾克、卡扎等层级治理，由总督、穆塔萨勒夫、卡伊马卡姆与法官共同管理。实际统治常依赖地方显贵、税务承包者、部族首领和宗教共同体领袖，阿拉伯诸省与巴尔干部分地区的中央控制程度并不均衡
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through provinces, sanjaks, and kazas, governed by pashas, mutasarrifs, kaymakams, and judges. In practice rule often relied on local notables, tax farmers, tribal chiefs, and communal leaders, and the degree of central control remained uneven in the Arab provinces and parts of the Balkans

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并行为伊斯兰教法、苏丹敕令法与各宗教共同体内部法。穆斯林事务主要由沙里亚法庭裁决，商业与行政事务越来越多受敕令和官署规章处理，非穆斯林社群在婚姻继承等方面保有内部自治
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic sharia, sultanic decree law, and the internal laws of religious communities. Muslim matters were handled chiefly in sharia courts, while commercial and administrative affairs were increasingly regulated by decrees and administrative practice, and non-Muslim communities retained internal autonomy in marriage and inheritance

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自地税、农业什一税、关税、盐烟等专卖或间接税、贡赋及各种杂捐。传统包税制仍广泛存在，但中央正试图加强直接征收与财政整顿，以应对军费和对外危机
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from land taxes, agricultural tithes, customs duties, monopoly or excise taxes on items such as salt and tobacco, tribute, and assorted levies. Tax farming remained widespread, but the center was attempting to strengthen direct collection and fiscal reorganization to meet military costs and external crises

religion.zh: 国家正统宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教（哈乃斐学派），但帝国内同时存在大量东正教、亚美尼亚使徒教会、天主教、犹太教、德鲁兹派及其他地方信仰共同体
religion.en: The state’s orthodox religion was Sunni Islam of the Hanafi school, yet the empire also contained large Orthodox Christian, Armenian Apostolic, Catholic, Jewish, Druze, and other local religious communities

philosophy.zh: 思想传统以伊斯兰政治法学、奥斯曼宫廷实用主义和乌里玛教育体系为主；到1840年前后，改革派官员 increasingly 吸收欧洲军事、行政与法制观念，为坦志麦特改革奠定思想基础
philosophy.en: Intellectual life rested on Islamic political jurisprudence, Ottoman court pragmatism, and the ulema educational tradition; by around 1840 reformist statesmen were increasingly absorbing European ideas of military organization, administration, and law, laying foundations for the Tanzimat

writingSystem.zh: 官方文字主要为奥斯曼土耳其语，采用阿拉伯字母书写；帝国内还广泛使用阿拉伯语、希腊语、亚美尼亚语、保加利亚语、塞尔维亚语、阿尔巴尼亚语和库尔德语等
writingSystem.en: The chief official language was Ottoman Turkish written in the Arabic script; Arabic, Greek, Armenian, Bulgarian, Serbian, Albanian, Kurdish, and many other languages were also widely used across the empire

culturalAchievements.zh: 奥斯曼文化以宏伟清真寺建筑、宫廷书法、细密画、宗教法学、诗歌、音乐与城市公共建筑著称。君士坦丁堡仍是伊斯兰世界与地中海之间的重要文化中心，印刷、翻译与近代教育机构虽有限但正在扩展
culturalAchievements.en: Ottoman culture was renowned for grand mosque architecture, court calligraphy, miniature painting, religious jurisprudence, poetry, music, and urban public works. Constantinople remained a major cultural center between the Islamic world and the Mediterranean, while printing, translation, and modern educational institutions were expanding though still limited

languageFamily.zh: 统治语言奥斯曼土耳其语属突厥语系，但帝国整体呈多语格局，涵盖闪米特语、印欧语、南高加索语等多种语系
languageFamily.en: The ruling language, Ottoman Turkish, belonged to the Turkic family, but the empire as a whole was profoundly multilingual, including Semitic, Indo-European, South Caucasian, and other language families

unit.zh: 百万国际元（1990年购买力估算）

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年奥斯曼帝国经济总量仍属欧亚非大国之一，但人均产出偏低，农业占主导，地区差异巨大。帝国经济深受战争、交通条件、地方割据和欧洲工业品竞争影响；同时港口贸易和部分原料出口在增长
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 the Ottoman Empire remained one of the larger economies of Eurasia and North Africa, but per-capita output was low and agriculture predominated, with major regional disparities. The economy was heavily affected by war, transport limitations, provincial fragmentation, and competition from European manufactures, though port trade and exports of certain raw materials were growing

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业为粮食农业、畜牧、棉花与丝绸生产、橄榄油、烟草、皮革、手工业纺织、地毯、金属工艺、造船、沿海贸易和商队贸易
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included grain agriculture, pastoralism, cotton and silk production, olive oil, tobacco, leather, artisanal textiles, carpets, metalworking, shipbuilding, coastal trade, and caravan commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 主要贸易品有谷物、棉花、丝、生丝、羊毛、皮革、烟草、鸦片、干果、橄榄油，以及进口的棉纺织品、金属制品、机械、糖和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Principal trade goods included grain, cotton, silk, raw silk, wool, leather, tobacco, opium, dried fruits, and olive oil, alongside imported cotton textiles, metal goods, machinery, sugar, and luxury products

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、手工具、住房和首饰。城市商人与地方显贵积累较多地产和现金，而广大农民与佃户资产有限，易受歉收、征税和战乱冲击
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, tools, dwellings, and jewelry. Urban merchants and provincial notables accumulated more real estate and cash, while most peasants and tenants possessed limited assets and were vulnerable to bad harvests, taxation, and warfare

unit.zh: 百万英镑（年贸易总额估算）
unit.en: million pounds sterling (estimated annual total trade)
